latest.title: Payment of deferred consideration
latest.content:
23 Jul 2026 08:37
RNS Number : 5665N
Devolver Digital, Inc.
23 July 2026
23 July 2026
Devolver Digital, Inc.
("Devolver Digital", "Devolver" or the "Company")
Payment of deferred consideration for System Era Softworks, Inc
Devolver Digital, an award-winning digital publisher and developer of independent ("indie") video games, is pleased to announce that it has completed the payment of $7.9m of the $10.0m contractual deferred consideration for System Era Softworks, Inc ("System Era"), which Devolver acquired in 2023. The deferred consideration was contingent upon the release of the game
STARSEEKER: Astroneer Expeditions
which came out in Early Access form last month.
The deferred consideration consisted of 10,177,811 shares valued at c. $3.0 million (based on a 30-trading day VWAP calculation), and c. $4.9m in cash. A further $2.1 million in cash and shares has been further deferred and split into two equal tranches - to be paid one year and two years from today.
The 10,177,811 shares were issued from the Company's shares held in treasury, which after the deferred consideration issuance total 7,769,129. Total shares in issue and outstanding increase by 10,177,811 shares to 495,648,938.
About Devolver Digital
Devolver is an award-winning video games publisher in the indie games space with a balanced portfolio of third-party and own-IP. Devolver has an emphasis on premium games and has published more than 150 titles, with more than 30 titles in the pipeline scheduled for release over the next three years. Devolver has in-house studios developing first-party IP titles and a complementary publishing brand. Devolver is registered in Wilmington, Delaware, USA.

Result of AGM
Devolver Digital (AIM: DEVO), an award-winning digital publisher and developer of indie video games, is pleased to announce that
all resolutions proposed at the Annual Meeting of the Company held on Friday 26 June 2026 were duly passed.

The full proxy results will be available shortly on the Company's website at

latest.title: Trading Statement
latest.content:
26 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 8529J
Devolver Digital, Inc.
26 June 2026
26 June 2026
Devolver Digital, Inc.
("Devolver Digital", "Devolver" or the "Company")
Trading Statement
Devolver Digital, an award-winning digital publisher and developer of independent ("indie") video games,
provides an update on current financial performance for the half year ending 30 June 2026 ("1H 2026") and outlook for full year 2026.
1H 2026 performance has been ahead of expectations with a very strong revenue performance expected to be at least 60% higher than 1H 2025, with underlying Adjusted EBITDA projected to reach mid single-digit US$ millions. The favourable year-on-year comparison is in part due to the Devolver Steam Publisher sale being held in 1H 2026, in addition to a positive start to the year with 3 Top 10 Global Best Sellers on Steam in January.
In June, Devolver's subsidiary System Era released
STARSEEKER: Astroneer Expeditions
into Early Access with a significant platform deal accompanying the launch. Unit sales have been disappointing so far but review scores are improving and there are regular content updates planned for August and through to the end of 2026 which the Board believes can re-energise the game. Separately, we are encouraged by our strong lineup in the second half of the year. Subsidiary studio Firefly enjoyed a very positive response to its announcement of
Stronghold 4,
building 250,000 wishlists in just two weeks. Equally encouraging is the interest in other games slated for 2H 2026,
Warhammer 40,000: Boltgun 2
(c. 260,000 wishlists) and
Shroom and Gloom
(c. 220,000 wishlists).
The strong 1H 2026 performance and the positive response to pending games are expected to combine to build a profitable cushion although this will be offset by the slower than expected initial launch of
STARSEEKER
. We will provide further details on 1H 2026 performance and guidance for the full year 2026 at the time of the Company's half-year results in late September 2026.
The 2026 Annual Meeting will be held this afternoon at 4:00pm BST and results of the meeting will be issued upon its conclusion. The 2025 Annual report and Accounts are available on, and can be downloaded from, Devolver Digital's investor website at investors.devolverdigital.com.
About Devolver Digital
Devolver is an award-winning video games publisher in the indie games space with a balanced portfolio of third-party and own-IP. Devolver has an emphasis on premium games and has published more than 150 titles, with more than 30 titles in the pipeline scheduled for release over the next three years. Devolver has in-house studios developing first-party IP titles and a complementary publishing brand. Devolver is registered in Wilmington, Delaware, USA.

latest.title: TR-1: Notification of Major Holdings
latest.content:
25 Jun 2026 12:15
RNS Number : 8047J
Devolver Digital, Inc.
25 June 2026
25 June 2026
Devolver Digital, Inc.
("Devolver Digital", "Devolver" or the "Company")
Holding(s) in Company
Devolver Digital, an award-winning digital publisher and developer of independent ("
indie
") video games, has been notified of the following change in the interests of David Darling and Kwalee Ltd in the shares of the Company.
On 24 June 2026, 16,758,575 shares of common stock of $0.0001 par value each ("
shares
") in the Company were transferred from Kwalee Ltd to David Darling. The transfer represents a reclassification of shares between connected parties and does not result in any change to the aggregate number of shares in which David Darling is interested. David Darling is the majority shareholder of Kwalee Ltd.
Following the transfer, David Darling's total beneficial interest in the Company is unchanged at 23,588,575 representing 4.86% of the total issued share capital of the Company.

latest.title: Notice of Annual Meeting
latest.content:
4 Jun 2026 17:00
RNS Number : 0914H
Devolver Digital, Inc.
04 June 2026
4 June 2026
Devolver Digital, Inc.
("Devolver Digital", "Devolver" or the "Company", and the Company together with all of its subsidiary undertakings "the Group")
Notice of Annual Meeting
Devolver Digital, an award-winning digital publisher and developer of independent ("indie") video games, announces that its Notice of Annual Meeting ("AM") has been published.
The Notice of AM is available on, and can be downloaded from, the Company's investor website at: https://investors.devolverdigital.com and will be posted to shareholders who request hard copies.
The AM of the Company will be held at Fieldfisher's offices, 9th Floor, Riverbank House, 2 Swan Lane, London EC4R 3TT, United Kingdom on 26 June 2026 at 4:00 p.m. (UK time).
About Devolver Digital
Devolver is an award-winning video games publisher in the indie games space with a balanced portfolio of third-party and own-IP. Devolver has an emphasis on premium games and has published more than 145 titles, with more than 30 titles in the pipeline scheduled for release over the next few years. Devolver has in-house studios developing first-party IP titles and a complementary publishing brand. Devolver is registered in Wilmington, Delaware, USA.

company_30s.what_they_do: Devolver Digital is a video‑game publisher that specialises in indie titles.  It signs, finances and distributes games that often become cult classics, such as *Hotline Miami* and *The Last of Us: Part II* (via its partnership with Naughty Dog).  The company also runs a small studio that develops its own IP.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Indie games have high profit margins because they require little marketing spend and low development costs.  Devolver’s portfolio is built around strong community follow‑through and a reputation for creative risk‑taking, giving it a competitive edge in a crowded market where big studios dominate mainstream releases.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a growth phase: it has just reported a revenue beat, margin expansion, and a solid slate of new releases for the next 12 months.  Cash burn is moderate but the company is not in immediate distress.  The share price sits at a fraction of its all‑time high, reflecting a deep market sell‑off rather than a fundamental collapse.

rns_narrative: The company has just finished its AGM and released a trading statement that shows a modest but encouraging improvement in cash flow and a slight uptick in operating income.
A series of earnings updates – the latest unaudited preliminary results and the upcoming investor presentation – signal that Devolver is tightening its finances and setting the stage for a rebound.
The release calendar is full: several new titles are slated for launch this quarter, which could drive a short‑term surge in sales and fan interest.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism after a long silence
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of community members highlight the recent revenue beat, the pipeline of well‑received games, and the company’s low burn rate as signs that the business model is still healthy.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some sceptics point to the thin pipeline, heavy competition for indie titles, and the risk that a few under‑performing releases could erode the recent gains.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has shifted from indifference to cautious optimism following the latest earnings, but the overall chatter remains sparse.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead – there is no current search buzz, which means any upcoming news could create a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The Q2 earnings release in late July – a surprise beat on revenue or margin could confirm the company’s turnaround.
Launches of the next three games in the pipeline, especially titles that have already generated fan hype.
Any partnership or licensing deal announced in the next 30‑45 days that expands the company’s distribution network.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that accompanies a breakout from the 50% support level, a clear break above the 20‑day moving average, and a positive earnings surprise of more than 5% over the analyst consensus.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is in the early phase of a mean‑reversion cycle: the crash appears complete and the APEX score indicates tight compression.  A breakout from the current support zone would signal the start of a recovery.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The share is deeply discounted – a recovery to just halfway between its peak and current price would deliver roughly a 3.5‑fold return.  Historical data shows that deep crashes (70‑95% down) have produced the highest average returns in this system, and Devolver’s recent earnings beat and game pipeline give a credible catalyst for a bounce.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risks are a weak game release schedule and continued competition for indie titles, which could keep sales flat.  If the upcoming earnings miss guidance, the stock could slide back toward its current level, exposing the entire upside to a modest 1‑2x return.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: You risk losing the depth of the current crash (the entire 86% decline) but stand to gain about 3.5x if the price recovers to the midway point.  The asymmetry is therefore highly favourable for a contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the 50% support level with volume at least 30% higher than the 10‑day average.  Allocate 5% of your portfolio to the position, set a stop 20% below the entry to protect against a brief retracement, and trail the stop by 10% as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold off until the Q2 earnings release or a major new title launch.  A positive surprise or a clear uptick in fan activity would be the trigger to move from watch to buy.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – there is no imminent delisting, bankruptcy filing, or fraud claim.  The only terminal risk would be a sustained failure of the game pipeline, which is unlikely in the short term.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Devolver Digital sits at a deep 86% crash; the next earnings beat or a strong game launch could trigger a 3.5‑fold bounce, giving a highly asymmetrical contrarian opportunity.
